title: 10 medicinal constituents of Coleus forskohlii (forskolin)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Coleus forskohlii forskolin is a plant-based compound extracted from the roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant, a member of the mint family. Forskolin has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various health conditions, including respiratory and cardiovascular issues. It has also gained popularity as a weight loss supplement and athletic performance enhancer due to its ability to increase muscle mass and improve overall physical fitness.

Coleus forskohlii diterpenes is a type of bioactive compound found in the leaves of the Coleus forskohlii plant. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties and is now widely researched for its potential health benefits. Forskolin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antihypertensive effects, as well as improving cardiovascular health by relaxing blood vessels and reducing blood pressure.

Coleus forskohlii cyclic AMP (cAMP) is a naturally occurring compound extracted from the plant. It acts as a cAMP activator, increasing the production of this molecule within cells. cAMP plays a crucial role in various cellular processes, including muscle relaxation, increased thyroid hormone secretion, and lipolysis. By elevating cAMP levels, Coleus forskohlii may help improve cardiovascular health, weight management, and overall metabolic function, making it a popular ingredient in dietary supplements and research studies.

Coleus forskohlii 7α-hydroxyforskolin is a bioactive compound extracted from the roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its therapeutic benefits, including cardiovascular and respiratory health. The compound works by increasing cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP) levels in cells, which can lead to various physiological effects such as relaxation of smooth muscles and decreased inflammation.

Coleus forskohlii forskohliquinone is a naturally occurring compound found in the roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions such as respiratory issues, digestive problems, and cardiovascular disease. Forskohliquinone works by stimulating adenylate cyclase, an enzyme that increases cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP) levels in the body, which can help to relax muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve heart function.

Coleus forskohlii dihydroforskolin is a potent herbal extract derived from the roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and enhancing respiratory function. As a dihydroforskolin supplement, it is believed to have enhanced bioavailability and potency compared to regular forskolin supplements, making it a popular choice among those seeking natural remedies for chronic conditions.

Coleus forskohlii 6β-o-acetylforskolin is a novel derivative of forskolin, which is extracted from the plant Coleus forskohlii. It possesses a unique structure featuring an acetyl group attached to the C-6 position of the forskolin molecule. This modification enhances its bioavailability and potency, making it a potential therapeutic agent for treating various diseases, including cardiovascular disorders, respiratory issues, and even cancer.

Coleus forskohlii neotigogens is a natural compound derived from the roots of the plant. It has been widely used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including improving cardiovascular and respiratory health, treating digestive issues, and even aiding in weight loss. Neotigogens has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and may also help reduce blood pressure and cholesterol levels.

Coleus forskohlii tigogenin is a plant-based compound derived from the roots of the Indian coleus plant (Coleus forskohlii). Tigogenin is a saponin glycoside that has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for various purposes, including treating respiratory and cardiovascular disorders. It has also been studied for its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a promising natural ingredient in modern medicine.

Coleus forskohlii neo-tigogenin is a bioactive compound derived from the root of the Indian plant Coleus forskohlii. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory and cardiovascular disorders. Neo-tigogenin has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a potential therapeutic agent for the treatment of various diseases.

intro: Coleus forskohlii has active constituents such as cinnamic acid, ferulic acid, and forskolin. These constituents have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, making Coleus forskohlii a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Forskolin, in particular, has been shown to improve cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels, while also treating respiratory issues such as asthma. Additionally, cinnamic acid and ferulic acid have been found to exhibit anti-cancer properties, potentially improving the quality of life for individuals suffering from various cancers.
intro_desc: Coleus forskohlii has active constituents such as forskolin, which is a labdane diterpenoid. These constituents have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and anti-asthmatic properties, improving cardiovascular health and reducing blood pressure. Forskolin's ability to stimulate the production of cyclic AMP (cAMP) can enhance fat burning, making it beneficial for weight loss and obesity management. This can improve people's quality of life by reducing the risk of chronic diseases.
